title: GuardDuty Finding Ingestion
spl:
index=aws sourcetype="aws:cloudwatch:guardduty"
| spath output=severity path=detail.severity
| spath output=finding_type path=detail.type
| where severity >= 7
| table _time finding_type severity detail.title detail.description
| sort -severity
description: GuardDuty provides ML-powered threat detection for AWS accounts. Centralizing findings in Splunk enables correlation with other security data.
value: GuardDuty provides ML-powered threat detection for AWS accounts. Centralizing findings in Splunk enables correlation with other security data.
grandmaExplanation: GuardDuty provides ML-powered threat detection for AWS accounts ‚Äî so risky changes, runaway spending, and suspicious logins do not go unnoticed.
implementation: Enable GuardDuty in all regions. Configure CloudWatch Events rule to forward findings to an SNS topic or S3. Ingest via Splunk_TA_aws. Alert on High/Critical findings (severity ‚â•7).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_aws`.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=aws:cloudwatch:guardduty`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Enable GuardDuty in all regions. Configure CloudWatch Events rule to forward findings to an SNS topic or S3. Ingest via Splunk_TA_aws. Alert on High/Critical findings (severity ‚â•7).

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=aws sourcetype="aws:cloudwatch:guardduty"
| spath output=severity path=detail.severity
| spath output=finding_type path=detail.type
| where severity >= 7
| table _time finding_type severity detail.title detail.description
| sort -severity
```

Understanding this SPL

**GuardDuty Finding Ingestion** ‚Äî GuardDuty provides ML-powered threat detection for AWS accounts. Centralizing findings in Splunk enables correlation with other security data.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=aws:cloudwatch:guardduty`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_aws`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: aws; **sourcetype**: aws:cloudwatch:guardduty. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=aws, sourcetype="aws:cloudwatch:guardduty". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Extracts structured paths (JSON/XML) with `spath`.
‚Ä¢ Extracts structured paths (JSON/XML) with `spath`.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where severity >= 7` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **GuardDuty Finding Ingestion**): table _time finding_type severity detail.title detail.description
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table by severity, Bar chart (finding types), Trend line (findings over time), Single value.
visualization: Table by severity, Bar chart (finding types), Trend line (findings over time), Single value.
